arts.ttl: 'Carnage' - The Hull City assessment that nails Tim Walter's heart-attack football - Hull Live
arts.sum: EFL pundit Adrian Clarke has praised Hull City and manager Tim Walter after watching the Tigers earn a 3-1 win at QPR on Tuesday night to notch up three straight victories in the Championship. City scored twice in the first half through Cody Drameh - his first in Black and Amber - and Chris Bedia before Rangers pulled one back from the penalty spot after Lewie Coyle was penalised for handball, but the game was put to bed 20 minutes from time when the excellent Liam Millar rattled in his first goal for the club.

arts.ttl: Significant Premier League transfer rule change that could impact Hull City
arts.sum: Previously, the EFL has worked with the Premier League to ensure symmetry across the leagues with this summer's window closing on August 30, at 11pm, but an August 15 start date would still come a week into the EFL season, which is expected to begin the weekend before around August 9 and 10. Managers across all leagues have voiced frustration previously about the window closing after the start of the season, including City boss Tim Walter, who felt it should be shut before the season kicked off.

arts.ttl: ‘Maybe’ – Hull City boss Tim Walter coy on new boy’s chances of debut against Norwich City
arts.sum: Hull City boss Tim Walter has said he is yet to decide whether to put Charlie Hughes into… The post ‘Maybe’ – Hull City boss Tim Walter coy on new boy’s chances of debut against Norwich City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Hull City head coach Tim Walter hands his side big Norwich City challenge
arts.sum: The Tigers boss admits to being impressed with the way his players have impacted games off the bench in the past three wins, something he says is important, but now possible given the depth of his squad, which is giving him reason to be excited about what could follow over the coming weeks. "If we're playing like we did in the last three weeks, and also against Sheffield (United), then I'm very excited to travel to Norwich to get these three points because it's also the next step for us, to try and keep this flow and that's important because afterwards we have a national break and it's always a good sign to go with three points into the break."

arts.ttl: Hull City's next three games will be acid test of summer transfer strategy
arts.sum: In those three wins against opposition, you'd expect to be towards the bottom half of the table, based on what we've seen in the opening weeks of the season, City have conceded 51 chances on their goal. Three wins in a row with 10 goals scored have made a big difference to the feeling around the MKM Stadium, both inside and out with players and fans having smiles as wide as the River Humber.

arts.ttl: Hull City injury latest as Tim Walter ponders Norwich City changes with Joao Pedro call
arts.sum: "Maybe, he was in full training, so maybe he can be or we are waiting until after the national break, but everything is possible, don't know at the moment," Walter said of the young defender. Elsewhere, the Tigers boss has been given a clean bill of health for the trip to Norfolk, with all those involved at Loftus Road on Tuesday night coming through without any issues, meaning only Dogukan Sinik, Ryan Longman, Andy Smith and young goalkeepers Thimothee Lo-Tutala and Harvey Cartwright are sidelined through injury.

arts.ttl: Marcondes close to Norwich deal - reports
arts.sum: Emiliano Marcondes is close to becoming a Norwich City player, according to reports from his native Denmark.
